fact: 492 people died in the Cocoanut Grove fire
source: National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) — Cocoanut Grove Nightclub Fire

fact: Approximately 1,000 people were packed inside the club (well over its legal capacity)
source: "Cocoanut Grove" entry, Britannica; official capacity was approximately 460, with an estimated 1,000 patrons present

fact: A busboy lit a match to find/replace a light bulb, igniting the fire
source: "Holocaust! The Shocking Story of the Boston Cocoanut Grove Fire" by Paul Benzaquin (1959); also widely documented in NFPA reports

fact: Exit doors were locked or bolted, and the main entrance was a single revolving door
source: National Fire Protection Association investigation report; detailed in Casey Grant, "The Cocoanut Grove Nightclub Fire," NFPA Journal (2017)

fact: The interior was decorated with flammable fake silk, paper palm trees, and other non-fireproofed materials
source: "Fire in the Grove: The Cocoanut Grove Tragedy and Its Aftermath" by John C. Esposito (2005)

fact: The fire spread with extreme rapidity — often described as killing hundreds within minutes
source: Widely documented; contemporary Boston Globe reporting (Nov 29, 1942) and NFPA analysis describe the fire engulfing the club in roughly five minutes, though 'fifteen minutes' is a common approximation in popular accounts
